name: Del Boy's Drinks - The Definitive Dataset
description: Every drink ordered, mentioned or served in Only Fools and Horses (1981-2003). Verified against original scripts and broadcast footage.

methodology.primary_source: Original episode scripts (66 files covering all 64 episodes)
methodology.priority: 1. Broadcast dialogue, 2. DVD subtitles, 3. Scripts, 4. Fan transcripts
notes: Scripts sometimes differ from broadcast - e.g. Baileys and Cherryade was Manhattan in the script but ad-libbed by David Jason
Drinks appearing in multiple episodes are listed once with an episodes array
Fan-reported drinks without script or broadcast evidence are listed as candidates

drinks.name: Caribbean Stallion
drinks.notes: The full recipe: tequila, coconut rum, Crème de Menthe, a smidgin of Campari, Angostura bitters, topped with grapefruit juice. Cost £7.

drinks.name: Tia Maria and Lucozade

episodes.title: To Hull and Back
episodes.note: 'He doesn't know what it is, but he likes it.'

episodes.title: Time on Our Hands
episodes.note: Del pours himself one at the cocktail bar. Still his go-to.

drinks.notes: One of the signature Del drink gags. Appears in both the 1985 special and the 2003 finale.

drinks.name: Tequila Sunset
drinks.notes: Del's homemade cocktail - actually made with gin because he ran out of tequila.

drinks.name: Pina Colada

episodes.title: Homesick
episodes.note: 'Tea, coffee, Pina Colada?'

episodes.title: A Slow Bus to Chingford
episodes.note: Grandad tried to get one but they'd sold out - got him a Mackeson instead

episodes.title: Danger UXD
episodes.note: 'pina colada for me, please, Michael'

episodes.title: Sickness and Wealth
episodes.note: 'A pina colada for me Nerys'

episodes.title: The Jolly Boys' Outing
episodes.note: 'a couple of pina coladas'

episodes.title: Miami Twice
episodes.note: fourteen pina coladas mentioned

drinks.notes: Del's most frequently ordered drink across the entire series.

drinks.name: Pina Colada with Ice and Alka-Seltzer
drinks.notes: After a heavy night: 'Juan. Can I have a pina colada with ice and Alka Seltzer?'

drinks.name: Baileys and Cherryade
drinks.notes: Del orders at the Nag's Head. Script says Manhattan but the broadcast has Baileys and Cherryade - David Jason ad-libbed the drink order.

drinks.name: Malibu and Cherryade
drinks.notes: 'a Malibu and cherryade for moi, and one for yourself.'

drinks.name: Campari and Diet Coke
drinks.notes: Del's usual: 'Yeah, Campari and diet coke please!'

drinks.name: Singapore Sling

episodes.title: Happy Returns
episodes.note: 'a Singapore Sling and half of Strongbow'

episodes.title: Dates
episodes.note: 'a Singapore sling for me and a small rum for Elton John'

episodes.title: Modern Men
episodes.note: Part of Del's big celebration round

drinks.notes: One of Del's recurring sophisticated orders.

drinks.name: Manhattan

episodes.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
episodes.note: 'giss a Manhattan Mike, small rum for him. Spit in the rum.'

episodes.title: Heroes and Villains
episodes.note: 'for Rodney and a Manhattan for moi.'

drinks.notes: Ordered in two separate episodes.

drinks.name: Beaujolais Nouveau
drinks.notes: Del orders 'a bottle of Beaujolais Nouveau... a '79' - not realising Nouveau means this year's vintage.

drinks.name: Spritzer ("Spitzer")
drinks.notes: Del's yuppie phase: 'I never liked Spitzers before, but now I'm right into 'em.' Scene of the famous bar flap fall.

drinks.name: Bacardi and Russian
drinks.notes: Barmaid lists Del's recent drinks: 'Tuesday it was a Bacardi and Russian.'

drinks.name: Grand Marnier and Orange
drinks.notes: 'last week it was Grand Marnier and Orange.'

drinks.name: Dubonnet and Coke
drinks.notes: 'the week before that was Dubonnet and Coke.'

drinks.name: Half a Lager
drinks.notes: Rodney's order after Del's elaborate Caribbean Stallion. The waiter mocks him: 'Half a lager... Reg Varney drank one of them in Holiday on the Buses!' - comparing Rodney to a naff 70s sitcom character.

drinks.name: Brandy and Andrews
drinks.notes: 'Del now stirs a spoonful of Andrews into his brandy.' Peak Del - medicating and drinking at the same time.

drinks.name: Rum and Black

episodes.title: A Royal Flush
episodes.note: Albert accidentally orders 'a bum and rack!'

episodes.title: Modern Men
episodes.note: Del orders 'A rum and Black for Bobby Crush' (Albert)

drinks.notes: Albert's drink - famously mispronounced.

drinks.name: Cuba Libre

episodes.title: Stage Fright
episodes.note: 'Blown it all on Cuba libres and lairy suits.'

episodes.title: Modern Men
episodes.note: Del says 'Cubre Libre' - his mangled pronunciation

drinks.notes: Del pronounces it 'Cubre Libre'.

drinks.name: Armagnac
drinks.notes: Pam offers Armagnac. Del: 'Yeah, that'll do if you're out of brandy.' - not realising Armagnac IS brandy.

drinks.name: Vin Ordinaire
drinks.notes: Del brags: 'I said get down that cellar and sort me out a bottle of Vin Ordinaire!' - thinking it means something posh.

drinks.name: Irish Coffee
drinks.notes: 'Del had a mutton Madras and four Irish coffees.' Four of them. Classic Del.

drinks.name: Peach Daiquiri
drinks.notes: 'Give us a peach daiquiri and a chipolata sandwich.'

drinks.name: Banana Daiquiri

episodes.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?

drinks.notes: 'giss a banana daiquiri for us moi and Australian lager for Jumbo.' Mike: 'I only sell British lager, Del.'

drinks.name: Blackcurrant and Pernod
drinks.notes: Ordered at the Nag's Head.

drinks.name: Rémy Martin with Cream Soda
drinks.notes: 'Oi John. Giss a Remi Martin with cream soda and lots of ice, and 'arf of lager.'

drinks.name: Chivas Regal and Coke

episodes.title: A Losing Streak
episodes.note: 'a large chivas Regal with coke!' Rodney orders Southern Comfort but Del overrides: 'That's 'alf a lager for Rodney.'

drinks.notes: Del orders defiantly: 'a large chivas Regal with coke!' Rodney orders Southern Comfort but Del overrides: 'That's 'alf a lager for Rodney.'

drinks.name: Tia Maria and Pineapple Juice
drinks.notes: 'Giss a Tia Maria and a pineapple juice and, er, 'alf a lager for lover boy.'

drinks.name: Rum and Pep
drinks.notes: Grandad recalls his mum: 'with her rum and pep in one hand, 20 Senior Service in the other.'

drinks.name: Drambuie with Lime, Soda, Ice, Lemon and Cherry
drinks.notes: Elaborate garnish-heavy order. Barmaid gives him two halves of lager instead.

drinks.name: Dubonnet
drinks.notes: Del tries to impress in Spain: 'Garçon, dos Dubonnet pore favore. Danke schon.'

drinks.name: Brandy and Cream Soda
drinks.notes: Wallace serves Del brandy but they've run out of cream soda.

drinks.name: Port and Lemon

episodes.title: Yesterday Never Comes
episodes.note: Del offers Miranda

episodes.title: The Frog's Legacy
episodes.note: Aunt Reen orders

drinks.notes: Offered by Del as a mixer option and ordered by Aunt Reen.

drinks.name: Grand Marnier and Grapefruit
drinks.notes: 'Yeah, Grand Marnier and grapefruit.'

drinks.name: Malibu and Tonic with Lime
drinks.notes: 'I'll have a Malibu and tonic with some lime and 'alf of lager, please darling.'

drinks.name: Courvoisier
drinks.notes: Del warns Albert: 'You'll feel even worse if I catch you at my Courvoisier again.'

drinks.name: Benedictine and Lemonade
drinks.notes: June's drink of choice - she totters in with a glass of it.

drinks.name: Bacardi and Coke
drinks.notes: 'Can I have a large Bacardi and coke, please?'

drinks.name: Malibu Reef
drinks.notes: Danny spots Del's abandoned drink: 'A Castella, a Malibu reef. Are you sure he's not around?'

drinks.name: Large Snowball

episodes.title: He Ain't Heavy, He's My Uncle

drinks.notes: 'How about a large snowball? Mike, a large snowball for Dora, please.'

drinks.name: Romanian Riesling
drinks.notes: Del's dodgy wine sold to the Nag's Head. Mike serves the remainder of the 'Romanian Rheisling.'

drinks.name: Tequila Slammer
drinks.notes: Mike lists the round: 'a G&T for Raquel, a Tequila Slammer for Rodney, orange juice for Cassandra.'

drinks.name: Mackeson
drinks.notes: Grandad's substitute: 'they'd sold right out of Pina Coladas Del, so I got you a Mackeson instead.' A dark milk stout instead of a cocktail. Del accepts it without complaint.

candidates.name: Harvey Wallbanger
candidates.notes: Strongly associated with Del's yuppie phase but NOT in any script. In Yuppy Love he actually orders Beaujolais Nouveau and Spritzers.

candidates.name: Creme de Menthe and Tizer
candidates.notes: Speculative. Tizer appears in scripts only as a comparison, never as a drink order.
